risks.risk_title: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery
risks.risk_description: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery in the Design Standards workstream. An upstream dependency remains uncertain, high priority, or not yet accepted by the downstream workstream. It is linked to related activity/activity group ACT-001 and related deliverable DEL-001. If this risk materialises, dependent activity/deliverable may slip if upstream item is late, unclear, or not signed off. This affects Design Standards and may compromise DEL-001, downstream sequencing, governance confidence, or readiness for the next delivery gate. Risk owner: Justin. Mitigation: Confirm upstream owner, due date, acceptance criteria, and escalation path. The mitigation must be owned by Justin, linked to DEL-001, and reviewed at each workstream checkpoint until the trigger condition is closed. Contingency: Re-sequence dependent work or escalate for resource/timeline decision. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Upstream dependency not completed by required date. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.

It is linked to related activity/activity group ACT-145 and related deliverable DEL-145. If this risk materialises, schedule/effort estimate may be inaccurate and requires validation. This affects Feature Delivery and may compromise DEL-145, downstream sequencing, governance confidence, or readiness for the next delivery gate. Risk owner: Wayne & Tebogo. Mitigation: Validate estimates and confirm contingency with delivery owner. The mitigation must be owned by Wayne & Tebogo, linked to DEL-145, and reviewed at each workstream checkpoint until the trigger condition is closed. Contingency: Re-baseline timeline if estimate variance is confirmed. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Estimate variance exceeds plan or confidence remains below 60%. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.

risks.risk_title: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery
risks.risk_description: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery in the Feature Delivery workstream. An upstream dependency remains uncertain, high priority, or not yet accepted by the downstream workstream. It is linked to related activity/activity group ACT-146 and related deliverable DEL-146. Contingency: Re-sequence dependent work or escalate for resource/timeline decision. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Upstream dependency not completed by required date. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.

risks.risk_title: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery
risks.risk_description: Unresolved upstream dependency may delay delivery in the Design Systems workstream. An upstream dependency remains uncertain, high priority, or not yet accepted by the downstream workstream. It is linked to related activity/activity group ACT-171 and related deliverable DEL-170. This affects Design Systems and may compromise DEL-170, downstream sequencing, governance confidence, or readiness for the next delivery gate. Risk owner: Brent van Zitters. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Upstream dependency not completed by required date. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.

This affects Execution and may compromise DEL-200, downstream sequencing, governance confidence, or readiness for the next delivery gate. Risk owner: To be confirmed. Mitigation: Clarify constraint, accountable party, and resolution date. The mitigation must be owned by To be confirmed, linked to DEL-200, and reviewed at each workstream checkpoint until the trigger condition is closed. Contingency: Escalate and re-plan if not resolved by next checkpoint. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Uncertainty remains unresolved. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.

It is linked to related activity/activity group ACT-233 and related deliverable DEL-225. If this risk materialises, schedule/effort estimate may be inaccurate and requires validation. This affects Publishing and may compromise DEL-225, downstream sequencing, governance confidence, or readiness for the next delivery gate. Risk owner: Bernice. Mitigation: Validate estimates and confirm contingency with delivery owner. The mitigation must be owned by Bernice, linked to DEL-225, and reviewed at each workstream checkpoint until the trigger condition is closed. Contingency: Re-baseline timeline if estimate variance is confirmed. The contingency should be activated if the trigger occurs or if mitigation progress is not evidenced by the next checkpoint. Trigger: Estimate variance exceeds plan or confidence remains below 60%. Treat this as active when the trigger is reached without evidence of closure.
